toolUseResult.originalFile:
## ============================================================================
## Trans / Non-binary comfort expressing political views analysis
## ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Source utilities
source("C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/scripts/survey_utils.R")
source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load and clean ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# Save cleaned/weighted version (preserving all columns)
write.csv(data, "harmonized_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ── 2. Recode variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Check actual comfort scale values
cat("\npol_speech_1 values:\n")
print(table(data$pol_speech_1, useNA = "ifany"))

# Harmonize pol_speech scales
# The general items use codes 1,3,4,5,6 (catalogue) but need to verify actual data
# Kirk items use codes 1,2,3,4,5
recode_comfort <- function(x, is_kirk = FALSE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  if (is_kirk) return(x)  # Already 1-5
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 1 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if 0-based export: 0,1,2,3,4)
    x == 3 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if catalogue-based: 1,3,4,5,6)
    x == 4 ~ 3,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 5 ~ 4,  # Comfortable
    x == 6 ~ 5,  # Very comfortable
    # Handle 0-based coding: 0,1,2,3,4
    x == 0 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_3),
    chilled_kirk_1_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_1, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_2_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_2, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_3_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_3, is_kirk = TRUE)
  )

# Gender identity variable (actual CSV coding, 0-based):
# gender1: 0=Man, 1=Woman, 2=Non-binary/Other, 3=Another way, 99=DK
# gender (Federal Post-Election): same coding
# gender2: 0=Not trans, 1=Yes trans, 99=DK
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    gender1_num = as.numeric(gender1),
    # Coalesce gender1 and gender columns (Fed Post-Election uses "gender")
    gender_identity = coalesce(gender1_num, as.numeric(gender)),
    gender_group = case_when(
      gender_identity == 0 ~ "Man",
      gender_identity == 1 ~ "Woman",
      gender_identity %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Non-binary / Other",
      gender_identity == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    is_trans = case_when(
      as.numeric(gender2) == 1 ~ "Transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 0 ~ "Not transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Combined IV: non-cis group
    gender_minority = case_when(
      gender_group == "Non-binary / Other" | is_trans == "Transgender" ~ "Trans / Non-binary",
      gender_group %in% c("Man", "Woman") & (is_trans == "Not transgender" | is.na(is_trans)) ~ "Cisgender",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nGender group distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_group)) %>% count(gender_group) %>% print()
cat("\nTransgender distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(is_trans)) %>% count(is_trans) %>% print()
cat("\nGender minority distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>% count(gender_minority) %>% print()

# ── 3. Pre/post Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025) ──────────────────────────

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    end_date = as.Date(EndDate),
    period = case_when(
      end_date < as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Pre-Kirk",
      end_date >= as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Post-Kirk",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nPeriod distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(period)) %>% count(period) %>% print()

# ── 4. Analysis: Comfort by gender minority status ──────────────────────────

# Reshape to long for the 3 comfort items
comfort_long <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, survey_name, survey_date, wt, gender_minority, period,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("pol_speech_"),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    item_label = case_when(
      item == "pol_speech_1_r" ~ "Express political\nopinions online",
      item == "pol_speech_2_r" ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      item == "pol_speech_3_r" ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

# Weighted means by group and period
means_by_group_period <- comfort_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, period, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Comfort by Gender Minority Status and Period ==\n")
means_by_group_period %>%
  arrange(item_label, gender_minority, period) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 5. Figure 1: Dot plot — comfort by gender identity × pre/post Kirk ──────

p1 <- ggplot(means_by_group_period,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = period)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 5),
    breaks = 1:5,
    labels = c("Not at all\ncomfortable", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very\ncomfortable")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Online",
    subtitle = "By gender identity, pre- and post-Charlie Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025)",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys (July 2024 – Feb 2026). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p1, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_by_gender_period",
          data = means_by_group_period)

# ── 6. Figure 2: Over-time trend of comfort by gender minority ──────────────

# Monthly weighted means
comfort_overtime <- comfort_long %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, survey_month, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

p2 <- ggplot(comfort_overtime,
             aes(x = survey_month, y = wtd_mean, color = gender_minority,
                 fill = gender_minority)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"), y = 4.8,
           label = "Kirk assassination\n(Sept 10, 2025)", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  facet_wrap(~item_label, ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Over Time",
    subtitle = "Cisgender vs. Trans / Non-binary respondents",
    x = NULL, y = "Weighted mean comfort (1–5)",
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys. Weighted. Shaded area = 95%% CI.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p2, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_overtime",
          data = comfort_overtime, height = 8)

# ── 7. Figure 3: N trans and non-binary respondents over time ────────────────

nb_trans_counts <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_group) | !is.na(is_trans)) %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(survey_name, survey_month) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_nonbinary = sum(gender_group == "Non-binary / Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_trans = sum(is_trans == "Transgender", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_either = sum(gender_minority == "Trans / Non-binary", na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_nonbinary = n_nonbinary / n_total * 100,
    pct_trans = n_trans / n_total * 100,
    pct_either = n_either / n_total * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

nb_trans_long <- nb_trans_counts %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_nonbinary, n_trans) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_nonbinary, n_trans),
               names_to = "group", values_to = "n") %>%
  mutate(
    group_label = case_when(
      group == "n_nonbinary" ~ "Non-binary / Other gender",
      group == "n_trans" ~ "Transgender"
    )
  )

p3 <- ggplot(nb_trans_long %>% filter(!is.na(survey_month)),
             aes(x = survey_month, y = n, color = group_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"),
           y = max(nb_trans_long$n[!is.na(nb_trans_long$survey_month)], na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
           label = "Kirk assassination", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-binary / Other gender" = "#FF8200",
                                "Transgender" = "#434E7C")) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Trans and Non-binary Respondents Per Survey",
    subtitle = "MEO tracking surveys, July 2024 – February 2026",
    x = NULL, y = "Number of respondents",
    caption = sprintf("Across %d surveys. Non-binary includes 'I describe myself another way'.\nTransgender from separate self-identification question.",
                      length(unique(nb_trans_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p3, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/nb_trans_counts_overtime",
          data = nb_trans_long)

# ── 8. Summary statistics table ──────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n== N per survey: trans/non-binary ==\n")
nb_trans_counts %>%
  arrange(survey_month) %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_total, n_nonbinary, n_trans, n_either,
         pct_nonbinary, pct_trans, pct_either) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 9. Kirk-specific comfort items (Oct 2025 survey only) ───────────────────

kirk_data <- data %>%
  filter(survey_name == "2025_10_Tracking", !is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, wt, gender_minority,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
         chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r)

kirk_long <- kirk_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
             chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    context = ifelse(str_detect(item, "kirk"), "About Kirk assassination", "General"),
    item_label = case_when(
      str_detect(item, "1_r") ~ "Express political\nopinions",
      str_detect(item, "2_r") ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      str_detect(item, "3_r") ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

kirk_means <- kirk_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, context, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Kirk-specific vs. General Comfort (Oct 2025) ==\n")
kirk_means %>% arrange(item_label, gender_minority, context) %>% print(n = 20)

p4 <- ggplot(kirk_means,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = context)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views: General vs. Kirk-Specific",
    subtitle = "October 2025 tracking survey",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s (Oct 2025). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(length(unique(kirk_data$ResponseId)), big.mark = ","))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p4, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/kirk_general_comparison",
          data = kirk_means)

cat("\n\nDone! All figures saved to analysis/figures/\n")

content.text: Now I also see that the pol_speech values are 0,1,2,3,4 (0-based), not 1,3,4,5,6. Let me fix the recode function and handle kirk columns that only exist in some surveys.

input.old_string:
# Harmonize pol_speech scales
# The general items use codes 1,3,4,5,6 (catalogue) but need to verify actual data
# Kirk items use codes 1,2,3,4,5
recode_comfort <- function(x, is_kirk = FALSE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  if (is_kirk) return(x)  # Already 1-5
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 1 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if 0-based export: 0,1,2,3,4)
    x == 3 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if catalogue-based: 1,3,4,5,6)
    x == 4 ~ 3,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 5 ~ 4,  # Comfortable
    x == 6 ~ 5,  # Very comfortable
    # Handle 0-based coding: 0,1,2,3,4
    x == 0 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_3),
    chilled_kirk_1_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_1, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_2_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_2, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_3_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_3, is_kirk = TRUE)
  )

# Actual data uses 0-based coding: 0=Not at all, 1=Not very, 2=Somewhat, 3=Comfortable, 4=Very
# Recode to 1-5 scale
recode_comfort_0based <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 0 ~ 1L,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 1 ~ 2L,  # Not very comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 3L,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 3 ~ 4L,  # Comfortable
    x == 4 ~ 5L,  # Very comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_3)
  )

# Kirk items (only in 2025_10_Tracking) — same 0-based coding
if ("chilled_kirk_1" %in% names(data)) {
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      chilled_kirk_1_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_1),
      chilled_kirk_2_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_2),
      chilled_kirk_3_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_3)
    )
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## ============================================================================
## Trans / Non-binary comfort expressing political views analysis
## ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Source utilities
source("C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/scripts/survey_utils.R")
source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load and clean ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# Save cleaned/weighted version to separate file
write.csv(data, "harmonized_cleaned.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ── 2. Recode variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Check actual comfort scale values
cat("\npol_speech_1 values:\n")
print(table(data$pol_speech_1, useNA = "ifany"))

# Harmonize pol_speech scales
# The general items use codes 1,3,4,5,6 (catalogue) but need to verify actual data
# Kirk items use codes 1,2,3,4,5
recode_comfort <- function(x, is_kirk = FALSE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  if (is_kirk) return(x)  # Already 1-5
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 1 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if 0-based export: 0,1,2,3,4)
    x == 3 ~ 2,  # Not very comfortable (if catalogue-based: 1,3,4,5,6)
    x == 4 ~ 3,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 5 ~ 4,  # Comfortable
    x == 6 ~ 5,  # Very comfortable
    # Handle 0-based coding: 0,1,2,3,4
    x == 0 ~ 1,  # Not at all comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort(pol_speech_3),
    chilled_kirk_1_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_1, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_2_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_2, is_kirk = TRUE),
    chilled_kirk_3_r = recode_comfort(chilled_kirk_3, is_kirk = TRUE)
  )

# Gender identity variable (actual CSV coding, 0-based):
# gender1: 0=Man, 1=Woman, 2=Non-binary/Other, 3=Another way, 99=DK
# gender (Federal Post-Election): same coding
# gender2: 0=Not trans, 1=Yes trans, 99=DK
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    gender1_num = as.numeric(gender1),
    # Coalesce gender1 and gender columns (Fed Post-Election uses "gender")
    gender_identity = coalesce(gender1_num, as.numeric(gender)),
    gender_group = case_when(
      gender_identity == 0 ~ "Man",
      gender_identity == 1 ~ "Woman",
      gender_identity %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Non-binary / Other",
      gender_identity == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    is_trans = case_when(
      as.numeric(gender2) == 1 ~ "Transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 0 ~ "Not transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Combined IV: non-cis group
    gender_minority = case_when(
      gender_group == "Non-binary / Other" | is_trans == "Transgender" ~ "Trans / Non-binary",
      gender_group %in% c("Man", "Woman") & (is_trans == "Not transgender" | is.na(is_trans)) ~ "Cisgender",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nGender group distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_group)) %>% count(gender_group) %>% print()
cat("\nTransgender distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(is_trans)) %>% count(is_trans) %>% print()
cat("\nGender minority distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>% count(gender_minority) %>% print()

# ── 3. Pre/post Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025) ──────────────────────────

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    end_date = as.Date(EndDate),
    period = case_when(
      end_date < as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Pre-Kirk",
      end_date >= as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Post-Kirk",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nPeriod distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(period)) %>% count(period) %>% print()

# ── 4. Analysis: Comfort by gender minority status ──────────────────────────

# Reshape to long for the 3 comfort items
comfort_long <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, survey_name, survey_date, wt, gender_minority, period,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("pol_speech_"),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    item_label = case_when(
      item == "pol_speech_1_r" ~ "Express political\nopinions online",
      item == "pol_speech_2_r" ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      item == "pol_speech_3_r" ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

# Weighted means by group and period
means_by_group_period <- comfort_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, period, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Comfort by Gender Minority Status and Period ==\n")
means_by_group_period %>%
  arrange(item_label, gender_minority, period) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 5. Figure 1: Dot plot — comfort by gender identity × pre/post Kirk ──────

p1 <- ggplot(means_by_group_period,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = period)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 5),
    breaks = 1:5,
    labels = c("Not at all\ncomfortable", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very\ncomfortable")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Online",
    subtitle = "By gender identity, pre- and post-Charlie Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025)",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys (July 2024 – Feb 2026). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p1, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_by_gender_period",
          data = means_by_group_period)

# ── 6. Figure 2: Over-time trend of comfort by gender minority ──────────────

# Monthly weighted means
comfort_overtime <- comfort_long %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, survey_month, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

p2 <- ggplot(comfort_overtime,
             aes(x = survey_month, y = wtd_mean, color = gender_minority,
                 fill = gender_minority)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"), y = 4.8,
           label = "Kirk assassination\n(Sept 10, 2025)", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  facet_wrap(~item_label, ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Over Time",
    subtitle = "Cisgender vs. Trans / Non-binary respondents",
    x = NULL, y = "Weighted mean comfort (1–5)",
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys. Weighted. Shaded area = 95%% CI.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p2, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_overtime",
          data = comfort_overtime, height = 8)

# ── 7. Figure 3: N trans and non-binary respondents over time ────────────────

nb_trans_counts <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_group) | !is.na(is_trans)) %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(survey_name, survey_month) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_nonbinary = sum(gender_group == "Non-binary / Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_trans = sum(is_trans == "Transgender", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_either = sum(gender_minority == "Trans / Non-binary", na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_nonbinary = n_nonbinary / n_total * 100,
    pct_trans = n_trans / n_total * 100,
    pct_either = n_either / n_total * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

nb_trans_long <- nb_trans_counts %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_nonbinary, n_trans) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_nonbinary, n_trans),
               names_to = "group", values_to = "n") %>%
  mutate(
    group_label = case_when(
      group == "n_nonbinary" ~ "Non-binary / Other gender",
      group == "n_trans" ~ "Transgender"
    )
  )

p3 <- ggplot(nb_trans_long %>% filter(!is.na(survey_month)),
             aes(x = survey_month, y = n, color = group_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"),
           y = max(nb_trans_long$n[!is.na(nb_trans_long$survey_month)], na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
           label = "Kirk assassination", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-binary / Other gender" = "#FF8200",
                                "Transgender" = "#434E7C")) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Trans and Non-binary Respondents Per Survey",
    subtitle = "MEO tracking surveys, July 2024 – February 2026",
    x = NULL, y = "Number of respondents",
    caption = sprintf("Across %d surveys. Non-binary includes 'I describe myself another way'.\nTransgender from separate self-identification question.",
                      length(unique(nb_trans_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p3, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/nb_trans_counts_overtime",
          data = nb_trans_long)

# ── 8. Summary statistics table ──────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n== N per survey: trans/non-binary ==\n")
nb_trans_counts %>%
  arrange(survey_month) %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_total, n_nonbinary, n_trans, n_either,
         pct_nonbinary, pct_trans, pct_either) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 9. Kirk-specific comfort items (Oct 2025 survey only) ───────────────────

kirk_data <- data %>%
  filter(survey_name == "2025_10_Tracking", !is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, wt, gender_minority,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
         chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r)

kirk_long <- kirk_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
             chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    context = ifelse(str_detect(item, "kirk"), "About Kirk assassination", "General"),
    item_label = case_when(
      str_detect(item, "1_r") ~ "Express political\nopinions",
      str_detect(item, "2_r") ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      str_detect(item, "3_r") ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

kirk_means <- kirk_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, context, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Kirk-specific vs. General Comfort (Oct 2025) ==\n")
kirk_means %>% arrange(item_label, gender_minority, context) %>% print(n = 20)

p4 <- ggplot(kirk_means,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = context)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views: General vs. Kirk-Specific",
    subtitle = "October 2025 tracking survey",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s (Oct 2025). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(length(unique(kirk_data$ResponseId)), big.mark = ","))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p4, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/kirk_general_comparison",
          data = kirk_means)

cat("\n\nDone! All figures saved to analysis/figures/\n")

lines: -# The general items use codes 1,3,4,5,6 (catalogue) but need to verify actual data
+# Actual data uses 0-based coding: 0=Not at all, 1=Not very, 2=Somewhat, 3=Comfortable, 4=Very

content.text: The analysis ran successfully. Let me view the figures.

All four figures are generated. Here's a summary of the results:

## Key Findings

**1. Trans/non-binary people are consistently less comfortable expressing political views** (Fig 1 & 2):
- Across all three items, trans/non-binary respondents score ~0.6-0.7 points higher on the discomfort scale (around "somewhat comfortable") compared to cisgender respondents (around "not very comfortable")
- The gap is persistent across the entire July 2024 – Feb 2026 period

**2. Pre- vs. post-Kirk assassination** (Sept 10, 2025):
- Cisgender respondents show a slight *decrease* in comfort post-Kirk (e.g., 2.20 → 2.13 for expressing opinions online)
- Trans/non-binary respondents show minimal change or slight increase post-Kirk, though CIs are wide given the small N (~117 post-Kirk)
- The Kirk-specific items (Oct 2025, Fig 4) show *everyone* is less comfortable specifically about Kirk-related political discussions, but the trans/non-binary gap persists

**3. N of trans/non-binary respondents over time** (Fig 3):
- Roughly 10-25 transgender respondents per survey wave
- Roughly 6-19 non-binary/other gender respondents per wave
- Combined (trans OR non-binary): ~17-36 per survey, ~500 total across all waves
- No clear trend change after the Kirk assassination

## Output Files
- `analysis/figures/comfort_by_gender_period.png` — Dot plot with CIs
- `analysis/figures/comfort_overtime.png` — Time series by group
- `analysis/figures/nb_trans_counts_overtime.png` — N over time
- `analysis/figures/kirk_general_comparison.png` — Kirk-specific vs general (Oct 2025)
- `harmonized_cleaned.csv` — Cleaned, weighted dataset (33,056 rows)
- `analysis.R` — Full reproducible script

**Caveat:** The trans/non-binary group is small (N~500 total, ~15-35 per wave), so wave-level estimates have wide CIs. The pre/post comparison pools across waves for more power.

) -> Path:
    """Get the survey directory from CLI override, env file, or catalogue metadata."""
    if override:
        p = Path(override).resolve()
    else:
        # Try env file
        if env_file:
            env_path = Path(env_file).expanduser().resolve()
            env = read_env_file(env_path)
            if "SURVEY_DIR" in env:
                p = Path(env["SURVEY_DIR"]).expanduser().resolve()
                if not p.exists():
                    print(f"Error: survey directory from env file does not exist: {p}", file=sys.stderr)
                    sys.exit(1)
                return p

        survey_dir = catalogue.get("survey_dir", "")
        if not survey_dir:
            print("Error: No --survey-dir provided, env file has no SURVEY_DIR, and catalogue.json has no 'survey_dir' field.", file=sys.stderr)
            print("Pass --survey-dir /path/to/surveys or --env-file /path/to/.survey-search.env", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)
        p = Path(survey_dir)
    if not p.exists():
        print(f"Error: survey directory does not exist: {p}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    return p


def extract_survey_data(
    survey_name: str,
    survey_meta: dict,
    survey_dir: Path,
    columns: list[str],
    extra_columns: list[str],
) -> pd.DataFrame | None:
    """Extract specified columns from a single survey's CSV file.

    Args:
        survey_name: Name of the survey
        survey_meta: Survey metadata from catalogue
        survey_dir: Root directory containing CSV files
        columns: Target variable columns to extract
        extra_columns: Additional columns (attention checks, demographics, etc.)

    Returns:
        DataFrame with extracted columns + survey metadata, or None on failure.
    """
    csv_rel = survey_meta.get("csv_file")
    if not csv_rel:
        print(f"  WARNING: No CSV file for survey '{survey_name}', skipping.")
        return None

    csv_path = survey_dir / csv_rel
    if not csv_path.exists():
        # Try resolving relative to the QSF file's directory
        csv_path = Path(csv_rel)
        if not csv_path.exists():
            print(f"  WARNING: CSV not found: {csv_rel}, skipping.")
            return None

    offset = survey_meta.get("data_row_offset", 2)

    # Read header to determine available columns
    try:
        header_df = pd.read_csv(csv_path, nrows=0, encoding="utf-8-sig")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  WARNING: Cannot read CSV header for '{survey_name}': {e}")
        return None

    available = set(header_df.columns)

    # Expand multi-select variables: if "interf_country" is not a column but
    # "interf_country_1", "interf_country_2", ... exist, include all of them.
    expanded_columns = []
    for col in columns:
        if col in available:
            expanded_columns.append(col)
        else:
            # Check for multi-select pattern: col_1, col_2, ...
            ms_cols = sorted(
                [h for h in available if h.startswith(col + "_")
                 and h[len(col)+1:].isdigit()],
                key=lambda x: int(x[len(col)+1:])
            )
            if ms_cols:
                expanded_columns.extend(ms_cols)
            else:
                expanded_columns.append(col)  # keep for missing-column reporting

    # Always try to include ResponseId
    meta_cols = ["ResponseId", "StartDate", "EndDate", "Finished",
                 "Q_TerminateFlag"]
    all_wanted = list(set(expanded_columns + extra_columns + meta_cols))
    use_cols = [c for c in all_wanted if c in available]

    if not any(c in available for c in expanded_columns):
        print(f"  WARNING: None of the target columns found in '{survey_name}', skipping.")
        return None

    try:
        df = pd.read_csv(
            csv_path,
            usecols=use_cols,
            skiprows=list(range(1, offset + 1)),  # skip description/import rows
            encoding="utf-8-sig",
            low_memory=False,
        )
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  WARNING: Failed to read '{survey_name}': {e}")
        return None

    # Add survey metadata
    df["survey_name"] = survey_name
    df["survey_date"] = survey_meta.get("date", "unknown")

    found_cols = [c for c in expanded_columns if c in available]
    missing_cols = [c for c in columns if c not in available
                    and not any(h.startswith(c + "_") and h[len(c)+1:].isdigit()
                                for h in available)]

content.text:
Now I see the issue. The Kirk items use **1-5 coding** (no custom recode), while the general items use **0-4 coding** (custom recode). Both go in the same direction (low = not comfortable, high = very comfortable), but on different scales.

My `recode_comfort_0based` function applied the same 0→1, 1→2, etc. shift to both. But the Kirk items are already 1-5, so they shouldn't be shifted. Let me fix this.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## ============================================================================
## Trans / Non-binary comfort expressing political views analysis
## ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Source utilities
source("C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/scripts/survey_utils.R")
source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load and clean ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# Save cleaned/weighted version to separate file
write.csv(data, "harmonized_cleaned.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ── 2. Recode variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Check actual comfort scale values
cat("\npol_speech_1 values:\n")
print(table(data$pol_speech_1, useNA = "ifany"))

# Actual data uses 0-based coding: 0=Not at all, 1=Not very, 2=Somewhat, 3=Comfortable, 4=Very
# Recode to 1-5 scale
recode_comfort_0based <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 0 ~ 1L,  # Not at all comfortable
    x == 1 ~ 2L,  # Not very comfortable
    x == 2 ~ 3L,  # Somewhat comfortable
    x == 3 ~ 4L,  # Comfortable
    x == 4 ~ 5L,  # Very comfortable
    TRUE ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    pol_speech_1_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_1),
    pol_speech_2_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_2),
    pol_speech_3_r = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_3)
  )

# Kirk items (only in 2025_10_Tracking) — same 0-based coding
if ("chilled_kirk_1" %in% names(data)) {
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      chilled_kirk_1_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_1),
      chilled_kirk_2_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_2),
      chilled_kirk_3_r = recode_comfort_0based(chilled_kirk_3)
    )
}

# Gender identity variable (actual CSV coding, 0-based):
# gender1: 0=Man, 1=Woman, 2=Non-binary/Other, 3=Another way, 99=DK
# gender (Federal Post-Election): same coding
# gender2: 0=Not trans, 1=Yes trans, 99=DK
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    gender1_num = as.numeric(gender1),
    # Coalesce gender1 and gender columns (Fed Post-Election uses "gender")
    gender_identity = coalesce(gender1_num, as.numeric(gender)),
    gender_group = case_when(
      gender_identity == 0 ~ "Man",
      gender_identity == 1 ~ "Woman",
      gender_identity %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Non-binary / Other",
      gender_identity == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    is_trans = case_when(
      as.numeric(gender2) == 1 ~ "Transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 0 ~ "Not transgender",
      as.numeric(gender2) == 99 ~ NA_character_,  # DK — exclude
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Combined IV: non-cis group
    gender_minority = case_when(
      gender_group == "Non-binary / Other" | is_trans == "Transgender" ~ "Trans / Non-binary",
      gender_group %in% c("Man", "Woman") & (is_trans == "Not transgender" | is.na(is_trans)) ~ "Cisgender",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nGender group distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_group)) %>% count(gender_group) %>% print()
cat("\nTransgender distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(is_trans)) %>% count(is_trans) %>% print()
cat("\nGender minority distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>% count(gender_minority) %>% print()

# ── 3. Pre/post Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025) ──────────────────────────

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    end_date = as.Date(EndDate),
    period = case_when(
      end_date < as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Pre-Kirk",
      end_date >= as.Date("2025-09-10") ~ "Post-Kirk",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("\nPeriod distribution:\n")
data %>% filter(!is.na(period)) %>% count(period) %>% print()

# ── 4. Analysis: Comfort by gender minority status ──────────────────────────

# Reshape to long for the 3 comfort items
comfort_long <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, survey_name, survey_date, wt, gender_minority, period,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("pol_speech_"),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    item_label = case_when(
      item == "pol_speech_1_r" ~ "Express political\nopinions online",
      item == "pol_speech_2_r" ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      item == "pol_speech_3_r" ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

# Weighted means by group and period
means_by_group_period <- comfort_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, period, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Comfort by Gender Minority Status and Period ==\n")
means_by_group_period %>%
  arrange(item_label, gender_minority, period) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 5. Figure 1: Dot plot — comfort by gender identity × pre/post Kirk ──────

p1 <- ggplot(means_by_group_period,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = period)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 5),
    breaks = 1:5,
    labels = c("Not at all\ncomfortable", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very\ncomfortable")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Online",
    subtitle = "By gender identity, pre- and post-Charlie Kirk assassination (Sept 10, 2025)",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys (July 2024 – Feb 2026). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p1, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_by_gender_period",
          data = means_by_group_period)

# ── 6. Figure 2: Over-time trend of comfort by gender minority ──────────────

# Monthly weighted means
comfort_overtime <- comfort_long %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, survey_month, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

p2 <- ggplot(comfort_overtime,
             aes(x = survey_month, y = wtd_mean, color = gender_minority,
                 fill = gender_minority)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"), y = 4.8,
           label = "Kirk assassination\n(Sept 10, 2025)", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  facet_wrap(~item_label, ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views Over Time",
    subtitle = "Cisgender vs. Trans / Non-binary respondents",
    x = NULL, y = "Weighted mean comfort (1–5)",
    caption = sprintf("N = %s respondents across %d surveys. Weighted. Shaded area = 95%% CI.",
                      format(nrow(comfort_long %>% distinct(ResponseId)), big.mark = ","),
                      length(unique(comfort_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p2, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/comfort_overtime",
          data = comfort_overtime, height = 8)

# ── 7. Figure 3: N trans and non-binary respondents over time ────────────────

nb_trans_counts <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_group) | !is.na(is_trans)) %>%
  mutate(survey_month = as.Date(paste0(survey_date, "-01"))) %>%
  group_by(survey_name, survey_month) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_nonbinary = sum(gender_group == "Non-binary / Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_trans = sum(is_trans == "Transgender", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_either = sum(gender_minority == "Trans / Non-binary", na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_nonbinary = n_nonbinary / n_total * 100,
    pct_trans = n_trans / n_total * 100,
    pct_either = n_either / n_total * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

nb_trans_long <- nb_trans_counts %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_nonbinary, n_trans) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_nonbinary, n_trans),
               names_to = "group", values_to = "n") %>%
  mutate(
    group_label = case_when(
      group == "n_nonbinary" ~ "Non-binary / Other gender",
      group == "n_trans" ~ "Transgender"
    )
  )

p3 <- ggplot(nb_trans_long %>% filter(!is.na(survey_month)),
             aes(x = survey_month, y = n, color = group_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2025-09-10"), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2025-09-10"),
           y = max(nb_trans_long$n[!is.na(nb_trans_long$survey_month)], na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
           label = "Kirk assassination", hjust = -0.05,
           size = 2.8, color = "grey40", family = "poppins") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-binary / Other gender" = "#FF8200",
                                "Transgender" = "#434E7C")) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Trans and Non-binary Respondents Per Survey",
    subtitle = "MEO tracking surveys, July 2024 – February 2026",
    x = NULL, y = "Number of respondents",
    caption = sprintf("Across %d surveys. Non-binary includes 'I describe myself another way'.\nTransgender from separate self-identification question.",
                      length(unique(nb_trans_long$survey_name)))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p3, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/nb_trans_counts_overtime",
          data = nb_trans_long)

# ── 8. Summary statistics table ──────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n== N per survey: trans/non-binary ==\n")
nb_trans_counts %>%
  arrange(survey_month) %>%
  select(survey_name, survey_month, n_total, n_nonbinary, n_trans, n_either,
         pct_nonbinary, pct_trans, pct_either) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# ── 9. Kirk-specific comfort items (Oct 2025 survey only) ───────────────────

kirk_data <- data %>%
  filter(survey_name == "2025_10_Tracking", !is.na(gender_minority)) %>%
  select(ResponseId, wt, gender_minority,
         pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
         chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r)

kirk_long <- kirk_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pol_speech_1_r, pol_speech_2_r, pol_speech_3_r,
             chilled_kirk_1_r, chilled_kirk_2_r, chilled_kirk_3_r),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "comfort"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort)) %>%
  mutate(
    context = ifelse(str_detect(item, "kirk"), "About Kirk assassination", "General"),
    item_label = case_when(
      str_detect(item, "1_r") ~ "Express political\nopinions",
      str_detect(item, "2_r") ~ "Respond to posts\nyou disagree with",
      str_detect(item, "3_r") ~ "Express opinions\nat work/school"
    )
  )

kirk_means <- kirk_long %>%
  group_by(gender_minority, context, item_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wtd_mean = weighted.mean(comfort, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    se = sqrt(sum(wt^2 * (comfort - weighted.mean(comfort, wt))^2) / sum(wt)^2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_lo = wtd_mean - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = wtd_mean + 1.96 * se
  )

cat("\n\n== Kirk-specific vs. General Comfort (Oct 2025) ==\n")
kirk_means %>% arrange(item_label, gender_minority, context) %>% print(n = 20)

p4 <- ggplot(kirk_means,
             aes(x = wtd_mean, y = item_label, color = gender_minority,
                 shape = context)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("Not at all", "Not very", "Somewhat", "Comfortable", "Very")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Cisgender" = "#434E7C", "Trans / Non-binary" = "#FF8200")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comfort Expressing Political Views: General vs. Kirk-Specific",
    subtitle = "October 2025 tracking survey",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = sprintf("N = %s (Oct 2025). Weighted estimates with 95%% CIs.",
                      format(length(unique(kirk_data$ResponseId)), big.mark = ","))
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot(p4, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/kirk_general_comparison",
          data = kirk_means)

cat("\n\nDone! All figures saved to analysis/figures/\n")

content.text:
**The scale is confirmed correct** via the QSF:
- 0 = "Not at all comfortable" → recoded to 1
- 4 = "Very comfortable" → recoded to 5

The Kirk-specific items now make more sense with the fix: for cisgender people, Kirk-context comfort (2.20) is slightly **lower** than general comfort (2.42) for expressing opinions online — i.e., people are a bit less comfortable in the Kirk context. That's the expected direction.

**The substantive finding stands: trans/non-binary respondents report being *more* comfortable expressing political views online than cisgender respondents** (mean ~2.8 vs ~2.2 on the 1-5 scale). This is counter to the initial hypothesis but actually aligns with some research on political engagement among marginalized groups — they may be more politically activated and willing to speak up, even if they face more risk.

Key updates:
- Feb 2026 now has 2,124 rows (was 573)
- Kirk items now correctly use 1-5 native coding (not shifted)
- Total N: 34,096 cleaned rows across 19 surveys

message.content: Can you run a linear model to see if trans and non-binary people still feel more comfortable expressing their views online when controlling for age, social media use, and political interest (are there other variables I should control for?). Should I use survey wave fixed effects or only cluster standard errors by wave?

content.text: Good questions. Let me first find the available control variables, then we can discuss the modeling strategy.

# Get full details on the best-coverage variables
python3 -c "
import json
with open('C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/data/catalogue.json', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    cat = json.load(f)

# Political interest slider (22 surveys, 2021-2026)
q = cat['questions']['q_how_interested_are_you_in_politics_generally_set_the_slide']
print('=== Political Interest (slider) ===')
print(f\"Surveys: {len(q['appearances'])}\")
print(f\"Choices: {q.get('choices', {})}\")
# Check which tracking surveys have it
tracking = [a for a in q['appearances'] if 'Tracking' in a['survey']]
print(f\"In tracking surveys: {len(tracking)}\")
for a in tracking[-5:]:
    print(f\"  {a['survey']} ({a['date']}): {a['variable']}\")

print()

# Social media news frequency
q2 = cat['questions']['q_now_thinking_about_the_news_you_get_on_your_smartphone_com__social_media_websites_and_apps_eg_xtwit']
print('=== SM news frequency ===')
print(f\"Surveys: {len(q2['appearances'])}\")
print(f\"Choices: {q2.get('choices', {})}\")
tracking2 = [a for a in q2['appearances'] if 'Tracking' in a['survey']]
print(f\"In tracking surveys: {len(tracking2)}\")

print()

# SM platform use frequency (how often do you use...)
q3 = cat['questions']['q_how_often_do_you_use_each_of_the_social_media_apps_and_web__facebook']
print('=== SM use frequency (Facebook as proxy) ===')
print(f\"Surveys: {len(q3['appearances'])}\")
print(f\"Choices: {q3.get('choices', {})}\")

content: I think we should include region fixed effect (Atlantic = Prince Edward Island, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, and New Brunswick; Quebec = Quebec, Ontario = Ontario, British Columbia = British Columbia, Prairies = Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba).

content.text:
Here's what's available in the tracking surveys for controls:

| Variable | Coverage | Variable name |
|----------|----------|--------------|
| Political interest (0-100 slider) | 16 tracking surveys | `pol_intrst` |
| SM use frequency (per platform) | 16 tracking surveys | `socialUse_1` (FB), `_2` (YT), etc. |
| Left-right ideology (0-10) | ~10 tracking surveys (3 different question keys) | `ideology` |
| Education, income, age, province | All surveys | Already extracted |

**On controls:** Beyond what you listed, I'd suggest adding **education**, **income**, and **province/region**. Ideology is tempting but has patchy coverage (only ~10 of 17 tracking waves), so it would cut the sample substantially. We could run it as a robustness check.

**On wave FE vs clustered SEs:** Both serve different purposes:

- **Wave FE** absorb all between-wave variation (sampling differences, political context, seasonality). This gives you the cleanest estimate of the gender minority comfort gap. But they also absorb the pre/post Kirk effect since each wave falls entirely in one period.
- **Clustered SEs by wave** correct for within-wave correlation without absorbing level differences, and let you include a `post_kirk` indicator.

**Recommendation:** Run two models:
1. **Main model** with wave FE — cleanest estimate of the trans/NB comfort gap, controlling for composition
2. **Interaction model** with `post_kirk × gender_minority` (no wave FE, cluster SEs by wave) — tests whether the gap changed after Kirk

Let me re-extract with the additional controls and build the models.

## ============================================================================
## Linear models: Trans/NB comfort expressing political views
## ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

source("C:/Users/math_/.claude/skills/survey-search/scripts/survey_utils.R")
source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load cleaned/weighted data ───────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# ── 2. Recode all variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Comfort: 0-based (0=Not at all, 4=Very comfortable) → 1-5
recode_comfort_0based <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 0 ~ 1L, x == 1 ~ 2L, x == 2 ~ 3L, x == 3 ~ 4L, x == 4 ~ 5L,
    TRUE ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    # DVs
    comfort_online = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_1),
    comfort_respond = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_2),
    comfort_work = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_3),
    # Gender identity (0=Man, 1=Woman, 2/3=NB/Other, 99=DK)
    gender_identity = coalesce(as.numeric(gender1), as.numeric(gender)),
    gender_group = case_when(
      gender_identity == 0 ~ "Man",
      gender_identity == 1 ~ "Woman",
      gender_identity %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Non-binary / Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    is_trans = case_when(
      as.numeric(gender2) == 1 ~ 1L,
      as.numeric(gender2) == 0 ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # Combined IV
    gender_minority = case_when(
      gender_group == "Non-binary / Other" | is_trans == 1 ~ 1L,
      gender_group %in% c("Man", "Woman") & (is_trans == 0 | is.na(is_trans)) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # Region FE (user-specified groupings)
    province_num = as.numeric(province),
    region = case_when(
      province_num %in% c(4, 5, 7, 10) ~ "Atlantic",
      province_num == 11 ~ "Quebec",
      province_num == 9 ~ "Ontario",
      province_num == 2 ~ "British Columbia",
      province_num %in% c(1, 3, 12) ~ "Prairies",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Controls
    age_num = as.numeric(age),
    pol_interest = as.numeric(pol_intrst_1),
    sm_use = as.numeric(socialUse_1),  # Facebook use frequency
    educ = as.numeric(education),
    inc = coalesce(as.numeric(income), as.numeric(Income)),
    # Period
    end_date = as.Date(EndDate),
    post_kirk = as.integer(end_date >= as.Date("2025-09-10")),
    # Wave as factor
    wave = factor(survey_name)
  )

# Comfort index: mean of the 3 items
data <- data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(comfort_index = mean(c(comfort_online, comfort_respond, comfort_work), na.rm = FALSE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ── 3. Descriptive stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n== Analysis sample ==\n")
analysis <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort_index), !is.na(gender_minority), !is.na(age_num),
         !is.na(region))
cat("Full sample (comfort + gender + age + region):", nrow(analysis), "\n")

analysis_full <- analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(pol_interest), !is.na(sm_use))
cat("With pol_interest + sm_use:", nrow(analysis_full), "\n")

cat("\nGender minority in analysis sample:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(gender_minority) %>% print()

cat("\nRegion distribution:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(region) %>% print()

cat("\nWave distribution:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(wave) %>% arrange(wave) %>% print(n = 30)

# ── 4. Model 1: Wave FE (main model) ────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 1: Wave FE — Comfort index\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m1 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
            factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis_full, weights = wt)

# Cluster SEs by wave for robustness
vcov_cl <- vcovCL(m1, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
ct1 <- coeftest(m1, vcov = vcov_cl)

cat("\nCoefficients (clustered SEs by wave):\n")
# Print only substantive coefficients (not wave FE)
coef_names <- rownames(ct1)
substantive <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", coef_names)
print(ct1[substantive, ])

cat("\nR-squared:", summary(m1)$r.squared, "\n")
cat("Adj R-squared:", summary(m1)$adj.r.squared, "\n")
cat("N:", nobs(m1), "\n")

# ── 5. Model 1b-1d: Individual comfort items ────────────────────────────────

for (dv in c("comfort_online", "comfort_respond", "comfort_work")) {
  cat("\n\n--- Model 1 variant:", dv, "---\n")
  f <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
                          factor(region) + factor(wave)"))
  m <- lm(f, data = analysis_full, weights = wt)
  vcov_m <- vcovCL(m, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
  ct <- coeftest(m, vcov = vcov_m)
  substantive_m <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct))
  print(ct[substantive_m, ])
  cat("N:", nobs(m), "\n")
}

# ── 6. Model 2: Post-Kirk interaction (no wave FE, clustered SEs) ───────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 2: Post-Kirk × Gender Minority interaction\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m2 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority * post_kirk + age_num + pol_interest +
            sm_use + factor(region),
          data = analysis_full, weights = wt)

vcov_cl2 <- vcovCL(m2, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
ct2 <- coeftest(m2, vcov = vcov_cl2)
print(ct2)

cat("\nR-squared:", summary(m2)$r.squared, "\n")
cat("N:", nobs(m2), "\n")

# ── 7. Model 2b-2d: Interaction for individual items ────────────────────────

for (dv in c("comfort_online", "comfort_respond", "comfort_work")) {
  cat("\n--- Model 2 variant:", dv, "---\n")
  f <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~ gender_minority * post_kirk + age_num + pol_interest +
                          sm_use + factor(region)"))
  m <- lm(f, data = analysis_full, weights = wt)
  vcov_m <- vcovCL(m, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
  ct <- coeftest(m, vcov = vcov_m)
  print(ct)
  cat("N:", nobs(m), "\n")
}

# ── 8. Model 3: Without pol_interest and sm_use (larger N) ──────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 3: Wave FE, no pol_interest/sm_use (larger sample)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m3 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis, weights = wt)

vcov_cl3 <- vcovCL(m3, cluster = analysis$wave)
ct3 <- coeftest(m3, vcov = vcov_cl3)
substantive3 <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct3))
print(ct3[substantive3, ])
cat("N:", nobs(m3), "\n")

# ── 9. Robustness: education and income controls ────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 4: Full controls (+ education + income)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

analysis_r <- analysis_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(educ), !is.na(inc))
cat("N with all controls:", nrow(analysis_r), "\n")

m4 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
            educ + inc + factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis_r, weights = wt)

vcov_cl4 <- vcovCL(m4, cluster = analysis_r$wave)
ct4 <- coeftest(m4, vcov = vcov_cl4)
substantive4 <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct4))
print(ct4[substantive4, ])
cat("N:", nobs(m4), "\n")

# ── 10. Coefficient plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Collect gender_minority coefficient from all models
extract_coef <- function(model, vcov_cl, label) {
  ct <- coeftest(model, vcov = vcov_cl)
  idx <- which(rownames(ct) == "gender_minority")
  tibble(
    model = label,
    estimate = ct[idx, 1],
    se = ct[idx, 2],
    ci_lo = ct[idx, 1] - 1.96 * ct[idx, 2],
    ci_hi = ct[idx, 1] + 1.96 * ct[idx, 2],
    p = ct[idx, 4],
    n = nobs(model)
  )
}

coef_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_coef(m3, vcov_cl3, "Age + Region + Wave FE"),
  extract_coef(m1, vcov_cl, "+ Pol. interest + SM use"),
  extract_coef(m4, vcov_cl4, "+ Education + Income")
)

coef_df$model <- factor(coef_df$model,
                        levels = rev(c("Age + Region + Wave FE",
                                       "+ Pol. interest + SM use",
                                       "+ Education + Income")))

p_coef <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = estimate, y = model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi), size = 0.6, color = "#FF8200") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("b = %.2f (N = %s)", estimate, format(n, big.mark = ","))),
            hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.8, size = 3, family = "poppins") +
  labs(
    title = "Trans / Non-binary Coefficient on Comfort Index (1–5)",
    subtitle = "OLS with survey weights and wave-clustered SEs",
    x = "Coefficient (comfort scale points)", y = NULL,
    caption = "Positive values = more comfortable. All models include wave and region FE."
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))

save_plot(p_coef, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/coef_plot",
          data = coef_df, width = 7, height = 3.5)

cat("\n\nDone! Models estimated and coefficient plot saved.\n")

## ============================================================================

library(lmtest)

library(stringr)

source("C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/ai_interns/templates/meo_theme.R")

# ── 1. Load cleaned/weighted data ───────────────────────────────────────────

catalogue <- load_catalogue()
data <- read.csv("harmonized_data.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM")
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(data), "\n")

data <- clean_survey_data(data, catalogue)
data <- weight_survey_data(data, catalogue, method = "rake")

# ── 2. Recode all variables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Comfort: 0-based (0=Not at all, 4=Very comfortable) → 1-5
recode_comfort_0based <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x == 0 ~ 1L, x == 1 ~ 2L, x == 2 ~ 3L, x == 3 ~ 4L, x == 4 ~ 5L,
    TRUE ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    # DVs
    comfort_online = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_1),
    comfort_respond = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_2),
    comfort_work = recode_comfort_0based(pol_speech_3),
    # Gender identity (0=Man, 1=Woman, 2/3=NB/Other, 99=DK)
    gender_identity = coalesce(as.numeric(gender1), as.numeric(gender)),
    gender_group = case_when(
      gender_identity == 0 ~ "Man",
      gender_identity == 1 ~ "Woman",
      gender_identity %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Non-binary / Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    is_trans = case_when(
      as.numeric(gender2) == 1 ~ 1L,
      as.numeric(gender2) == 0 ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # Combined IV
    gender_minority = case_when(
      gender_group == "Non-binary / Other" | is_trans == 1 ~ 1L,
      gender_group %in% c("Man", "Woman") & (is_trans == 0 | is.na(is_trans)) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # Region FE (user-specified groupings)
    province_num = as.numeric(province),
    region = case_when(
      province_num %in% c(4, 5, 7, 10) ~ "Atlantic",
      province_num == 11 ~ "Quebec",
      province_num == 9 ~ "Ontario",
      province_num == 2 ~ "British Columbia",
      province_num %in% c(1, 3, 12) ~ "Prairies",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Controls
    age_num = as.numeric(age),
    pol_interest = as.numeric(pol_intrst_1),
    sm_use = as.numeric(socialUse_1),  # Facebook use frequency
    educ = as.numeric(education),
    inc = coalesce(as.numeric(income), as.numeric(Income)),
    # Period
    end_date = as.Date(EndDate),
    post_kirk = as.integer(end_date >= as.Date("2025-09-10")),
    # Wave as factor
    wave = factor(survey_name)
  )

# Comfort index: mean of the 3 items
data <- data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(comfort_index = mean(c(comfort_online, comfort_respond, comfort_work), na.rm = FALSE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ── 3. Descriptive stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n== Analysis sample ==\n")
analysis <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(comfort_index), !is.na(gender_minority), !is.na(age_num),
         !is.na(region))
cat("Full sample (comfort + gender + age + region):", nrow(analysis), "\n")

analysis_full <- analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(pol_interest), !is.na(sm_use))
cat("With pol_interest + sm_use:", nrow(analysis_full), "\n")

cat("\nGender minority in analysis sample:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(gender_minority) %>% print()

cat("\nRegion distribution:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(region) %>% print()

cat("\nWave distribution:\n")
analysis_full %>% count(wave) %>% arrange(wave) %>% print(n = 30)

# ── 4. Model 1: Wave FE (main model) ────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 1: Wave FE — Comfort index\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m1 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
            factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis_full, weights = wt)

# Cluster SEs by wave for robustness
vcov_cl <- vcovCL(m1, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
ct1 <- coeftest(m1, vcov = vcov_cl)

cat("\nCoefficients (clustered SEs by wave):\n")
# Print only substantive coefficients (not wave FE)
coef_names <- rownames(ct1)
substantive <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", coef_names)
print(ct1[substantive, ])

cat("\nR-squared:", summary(m1)$r.squared, "\n")
cat("Adj R-squared:", summary(m1)$adj.r.squared, "\n")
cat("N:", nobs(m1), "\n")

# ── 5. Model 1b-1d: Individual comfort items ────────────────────────────────

for (dv in c("comfort_online", "comfort_respond", "comfort_work")) {
  cat("\n\n--- Model 1 variant:", dv, "---\n")
  f <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
                          factor(region) + factor(wave)"))
  m <- lm(f, data = analysis_full, weights = wt)
  vcov_m <- vcovCL(m, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
  ct <- coeftest(m, vcov = vcov_m)
  substantive_m <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct))
  print(ct[substantive_m, ])
  cat("N:", nobs(m), "\n")
}

# ── 6. Model 2: Post-Kirk interaction (no wave FE, clustered SEs) ───────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 2: Post-Kirk × Gender Minority interaction\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m2 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority * post_kirk + age_num + pol_interest +
            sm_use + factor(region),
          data = analysis_full, weights = wt)

vcov_cl2 <- vcovCL(m2, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
ct2 <- coeftest(m2, vcov = vcov_cl2)
print(ct2)

cat("\nR-squared:", summary(m2)$r.squared, "\n")
cat("N:", nobs(m2), "\n")

# ── 7. Model 2b-2d: Interaction for individual items ────────────────────────

for (dv in c("comfort_online", "comfort_respond", "comfort_work")) {
  cat("\n--- Model 2 variant:", dv, "---\n")
  f <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~ gender_minority * post_kirk + age_num + pol_interest +
                          sm_use + factor(region)"))
  m <- lm(f, data = analysis_full, weights = wt)
  vcov_m <- vcovCL(m, cluster = analysis_full$wave)
  ct <- coeftest(m, vcov = vcov_m)
  print(ct)
  cat("N:", nobs(m), "\n")
}

# ── 8. Model 3: Without pol_interest and sm_use (larger N) ──────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 3: Wave FE, no pol_interest/sm_use (larger sample)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

m3 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis, weights = wt)

vcov_cl3 <- vcovCL(m3, cluster = analysis$wave)
ct3 <- coeftest(m3, vcov = vcov_cl3)
substantive3 <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct3))
print(ct3[substantive3, ])
cat("N:", nobs(m3), "\n")

# ── 9. Robustness: education and income controls ────────────────────────────

cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("MODEL 4: Full controls (+ education + income)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

analysis_r <- analysis_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(educ), !is.na(inc))
cat("N with all controls:", nrow(analysis_r), "\n")

m4 <- lm(comfort_index ~ gender_minority + age_num + pol_interest + sm_use +
            educ + inc + factor(region) + factor(wave),
          data = analysis_r, weights = wt)

vcov_cl4 <- vcovCL(m4, cluster = analysis_r$wave)
ct4 <- coeftest(m4, vcov = vcov_cl4)
substantive4 <- !grepl("factor\\(wave\\)", rownames(ct4))
print(ct4[substantive4, ])
cat("N:", nobs(m4), "\n")

# ── 10. Coefficient plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Collect gender_minority coefficient from all models
extract_coef <- function(model, vcov_cl, label) {
  ct <- coeftest(model, vcov = vcov_cl)
  idx <- which(rownames(ct) == "gender_minority")
  tibble(
    model = label,
    estimate = ct[idx, 1],
    se = ct[idx, 2],
    ci_lo = ct[idx, 1] - 1.96 * ct[idx, 2],
    ci_hi = ct[idx, 1] + 1.96 * ct[idx, 2],
    p = ct[idx, 4],
    n = nobs(model)
  )
}

coef_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_coef(m3, vcov_cl3, "Age + Region + Wave FE"),
  extract_coef(m1, vcov_cl, "+ Pol. interest + SM use"),
  extract_coef(m4, vcov_cl4, "+ Education + Income")
)

coef_df$model <- factor(coef_df$model,
                        levels = rev(c("Age + Region + Wave FE",
                                       "+ Pol. interest + SM use",
                                       "+ Education + Income")))

p_coef <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = estimate, y = model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi), size = 0.6, color = "#FF8200") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("b = %.2f (N = %s)", estimate, format(n, big.mark = ","))),
            hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.8, size = 3, family = "poppins") +
  labs(
    title = "Trans / Non-binary Coefficient on Comfort Index (1–5)",
    subtitle = "OLS with survey weights and wave-clustered SEs",
    x = "Coefficient (comfort scale points)", y = NULL,
    caption = "Positive values = more comfortable. All models include wave and region FE."
  ) +
  theme_meo +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))

save_plot(p_coef, "C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/GitHub/trans_incident/analysis/figures/coef_plot",
          data = coef_df, width = 7, height = 3.5)

cat("\n\nDone! Models estimated and coefficient plot saved.\n")

content.text: 0-based coding: 0=Never through 6=Several times a day. Let me verify with the QSF, then update the model.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
qsf_path = 'C:/Users/math_/Dropbox/PC/Documents/MEO/Survey/2024_07_Tracking.qsf'
with open(qsf_path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
    qsf = json.load(f)
for elem in qsf.get('SurveyElements', []):
    if not isinstance(elem, dict): continue
    payload = elem.get('Payload')
    if not isinstance(payload, dict): continue
    tag = payload.get('DataExportTag', '') or ''
    if tag == 'socialUse':
        answers = payload.get('Answers', {})
        answer_order = payload.get('AnswerOrder', [])
        recode = payload.get('RecodeValues', {})
        print('Answers (SM use frequency):')
        for aid in answer_order:
            aid_str = str(aid)
            label = answers.get(aid_str, {}).get('Display', '?')
            recode_val = recode.get(aid_str, aid_str)
            print(f'  {recode_val} = {label}')
        break
"
